quiz.question:
It's Black to move. 

What's the winning method here?

answers.text: Triangulation (losing a tempo) with ...Kd7 or Kc7 so that the kings can face each other with White to move.

feedback.text:
Exactly! 

The white king has to stay near the b-pawn, so eventually it will have to go back to the 4th rank, where Black will be able to win the opposition.

distractors.text: ...b3, pawn sacrifice, to distract the king and invade more squares with our own king.

feedback.text: Neither plan of invasion works because the white king can just take the pawn and then defend our threats.

distractors.text: ...Kd6, taking the king over to the kingside where it will have better attacking chances.

feedback.text: The king can never make progress in the kingside. And if Black doesn't get the opposition, Black won't be able to move forward.
